Class Topology

java.lang.Object
io.graphite.api.internal.GraphAPI
io.graphite.api.Topology
public final class Topology extends GraphAPI
Provides topological ordering algorithms.

The Topology service computes a valid topological ordering of directed acyclic graphs (DAGs). If the graph contains a cycle, an exception is thrown.

TraversalResult order =
        graph.topology().topologicalSort();

Available Algorithms

  • DFS Topological Sort
  • Kahn's Algorithm

Typical Applications

  • Task scheduling
  • Dependency resolution
  • Build systems
  • Course prerequisite planning
Since:
2.0
Version:
2.0
Author:
Chinmay

  • Constructor Details

    • Topology

      public Topology(IGraph graph)

  • Method Details

    • dfs

      public TopologicalSortResult dfs()
      Computes a topological ordering of a directed acyclic graph (DAG) using depth-first search.

      A topological ordering arranges vertices so that every directed edge points from an earlier vertex to a later vertex.

      This algorithm is applicable only to directed acyclic graphs.

      Complexity

      • Time: O(V + E)
      • Space: O(V)
      Returns:
      the topological ordering
      Throws:
      NullGraphException - if the graph is null
      UnsupportedGraphTypeException - if the graph is undirected
      GraphCycleException - if the graph contains a cycle
      Since:
      2.0
      See Also:
      • kahn()

    • kahn

      public TopologicalSortResult kahn()
      Computes a topological ordering using Kahn's algorithm.

      Kahn's algorithm repeatedly removes vertices with zero incoming edges until all vertices have been processed.

      Complexity

      • Time: O(V + E)
      • Space: O(V)
      Returns:
      the topological ordering
      Throws:
      NullGraphException - if the graph is null
      UnsupportedGraphTypeException - if the graph is undirected
      GraphCycleException - if the graph contains a cycle
      Since:
      2.0
      See Also:
      • dfs()
